analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.tag_disclaimer: A transaction with several tags counts fully in each — slices can add up to more than the total.
analytics.ring_savings_kept: Of income kept as savings this month
analytics.out_of_range_body: {{count}} transaction(s) dated outside the event period.

analytics_info.burnup: Compares how much you've spent this month, day by day, to how much you had spent by the same day last month. If today's line is above last month's, you're spending faster than before.
analytics_info.monthly_spend: Total money spent in each of the last months, shown as bars. The thin line is the 3-month average — it smooths out one-off spikes so you can see if your spending is generally going up or down.
analytics_info.avg_weekday: Your average spending for each day of the week, averaged over the whole period. Useful for spotting habits, like spending more on weekends.
analytics_info.calendar_heatmap: A month calendar where each day is colored by how much you spent — darker means you spent more that day. Great for spotting expensive days at a glance.
analytics_info.net_cashflow: What's left after money in and out: income minus what you spent minus what you saved. A bar above zero means you ended the month with more money than you started; below zero means you spent more than you earned.
analytics_info.savings_rate: What share of your income you kept as savings this month.
analytics_info.cumulative_balance: Adds up your net cash flow month after month, so you can see your overall balance building up (or draining) over time, instead of just one month at a time.
analytics_info.savings_cumulative: The running total of everything you've put into savings, month by month. It only ever goes up (unless you count savings withdrawn as negative).
analytics_info.spend_by_payment: Shows how much you spent through each payment method (cash, card, transfer...) so you can see which one you rely on most.
analytics_info.amount_distribution: Groups your transactions by size — how many were under 5, under 10, under 25, and so on — so you can see whether most of your spending comes from many small purchases or a few big ones.
analytics_info.ant_spend: 'Ant spend' means the many tiny purchases (coffees, snacks...) that feel insignificant alone but add up over the month. Named after ants: small individually, big as a colony.
analytics_info.refunds: The share of your spending that came back to you as refunds. A higher percentage means more of what you paid ended up being returned.
analytics_info.tag_coverage_gap: The percentage of transactions that don't have any tag yet. Tags help you analyze spending by things like mood or context — untagged transactions are missed in those views.
analytics_info.spend_timeline: How the cost of this event built up over its duration, so you can see if spending was spread evenly or concentrated on a few days.
analytics_info.budgets_progress: The bar shows how much of your budget limit you've used. 'Projected close' estimates your total spend by the end of the period if you keep spending at the current pace — a warning appears if that's more than your limit.
analytics_info.category_donut: Splits your spending into categories, sized by how much you spent in each. Tap a slice (or its legend row) with an arrow to drill into its subcategories.
analytics_info.tags_donut: Splits your spending by tag. Since a transaction can have several tags, it can count fully under more than one — so the slices may add up to more than your total spending.
analytics_info.vs_3m_avg: How this month's spending compares to your average over the last 3 months. A positive number means you're spending more than usual, negative means less.
analytics_info.top_category: The category where you spent the most this month.
analytics_info.budgets_at_risk: How many of your active budgets are on pace to go over their limit if spending continues at the current rate.
analytics_info.projection: An estimate of your total spending by the end of the month, based on how much you've spent so far and how many days are left.
analytics_info.no_spend_streak: The number of days in a row, up to today, with no recorded spending.
analytics_info.mom: Month-over-month: how much your spending changed compared to last month.
analytics_info.yoy: Year-over-year: how much your spending changed compared to the same month last year.
analytics_info.transactions: The total number of transactions recorded in this period.
analytics_info.mean_ticket: The average amount per transaction (total spent divided by number of transactions). A few large purchases can pull this number up.
analytics_info.median_ticket: The 'middle' transaction amount: half your transactions are smaller than this and half are larger. Less affected by a few very large purchases than the average.
analytics_info.max_ticket: The single largest transaction in this period.
analytics_info.total_cost: The total amount spent on this event.
analytics_info.cost_per_day: The total cost of the event divided by how many days it lasted.

common.name: Name
common.default_warning: Editing a default item will remove its translation permanently.
common.delete_with_budgets: "{{name}}" has {{count}} budget(s) that will also be deleted. Continue?
common.delete_with_budgets_multi: Selected items have {{count}} budget(s) that will also be deleted. Continue?

categories.delete_warning: This will also delete all subcategories and any budgets linked to this category. Expenses will lose their category.
categories.delete_warning_budgets: This category has {{count}} linked budget(s) that will be deleted. Subcategories and expenses will also be affected.
categories.delete_with_budgets: "{{name}}" (and its subcategories) has {{count}} budget(s) that will also be deleted. Continue?
categories.delete_with_budgets_multi: Selected categories (and their subcategories) have {{count}} budget(s) that will also be deleted. Continue?

tags.delete_warning: Deleting this tag will remove it from all expenses.

tag_groups.delete_warning: Tags in this group will be moved to Ungrouped before deletion.
tag_groups.delete_message: Its tags will be moved to "Ungrouped" first, then "{{name}}" is deleted.

payment_methods.delete_warning: Deleting this payment method will remove it from all expenses.
payment_methods.delete_message: Delete "{{name}}"? Expenses using it will keep their data but lose the reference.

events.delete_warning: Deleting this event will remove it from all expenses. Any linked budgets will also be deleted.
events.delete_warning_budgets: This event has {{count}} linked budget(s) that will be deleted.

projects.delete_warning: Deleting this project will remove it from all expenses. Any linked budgets will also be deleted.
projects.delete_warning_budgets: This project has {{count}} linked budget(s) that will be deleted.

profile.currency_live_budget_warning: You have active budgets in this currency. Changing currency won't affect existing records.

recurring.title: Recurring
recurring.delete_message: Delete {{count}} recurring template(s)? Pending items will also be removed.

goals.per_month: Save {{amount}}/month to reach it in time

budgets.delete_warning: This budget will be permanently deleted.
budgets.type_monthly_hint: Recurs every month: this limit applies to each month on its own.
budgets.period_auto_hint: Fixed to the event or project duration.
budgets.entity_no_dates: This event or project has no start/end dates. Add them first to budget it.

backup.export_subtitle: Copies the local database and opens the share sheet
backup.restore_subtitle: Pick a .sqlite file — this overwrites all current data
backup.restore_confirm_message: This overwrites all current data with the selected backup. This cannot be undone. Continue?
backup.restored: Backup restored. Restart the app to see all changes.

expenses.delete_warning: This expense will be permanently deleted.
expenses.filter_partial: Filters apply to loaded expenses only. Load more to search further.
expenses.no_results: No expenses match the current filters.
